100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On May 21, 1926, Britain faced a nationwide general strike, as workers across various sectors halted operations in a protest against wage cuts and poor working conditions. In the United States, the aftermath of the recent Great Mississippi Flood continued to dominate headlines, with recovery efforts underway in the affected regions. Meanwhile, in Germany, tensions escalated as economic instability and political unrest persisted, following the Treaty of Versailles.
